meta.note: Full enriched data extracted from index.html for maintainability. Includes local county/city/school + statewide Governor + Ag Comm with detailed funding (specific donors, categories, limit flags), keyIssues, pollingSources (with skepticism), fundingNotes. Sources primary + secondary cited per entry. Scraper offices_up JSON provides dynamic 'offices up' list. Use for site + future pipeline. All entries require verification against official sources.
meta.disclaimer: Informational only. Verify at sarasotavotes.gov, votemanatee.gov, dos.fl.gov, county sites. Polling data shown with HEAVY skepticism. Funding from public records.

districts.name: Sarasota County Commission District 2

incumbent.name: Mark Smith
incumbent.notes: Architect, Siesta Key resident. First term. Vice Chair.

candidates.name: Mark Smith
candidates.funding: Development & business interests visible; raised ~2x challenger

candidates.name: Kristina Sargent (and others in primary context)
candidates.funding: TBD - monitor official filings (reported fundraising noted in coverage)

districts.fundingNotes: Siesta Key focus: Smith is long-time Siesta Key architect/business leader (Smith Architects in Village). History of dev funding but claims opposition to major Siesta hotels (requires supermajority). 2022: $53k+ from dev interests incl $9k Jensen. Budget up 71% on his watch. Suspicious vote against rescinding $7.5M to Jon Mast (critics allege conflicts). Outraised challenger Sargent 2x. Siesta Key community ties: past SKVA President, Chamber roles. Specific: active in SKA, SKVA beautification.
sources: Siesta Sand, local reports on community plan

districts.name: Sarasota County Commission District 4

candidates.name: Dr. Joe Neunder
candidates.funding: Significant developer support via committees (~20% in 2022)

topContributors.name: Rex Jensen, Hugh Culverhouse, Randy Benderson - $95k total to committee
Chris Brown network LLCs - multiple $1k post-vote (e.g. Siesta Key Summer House)

limitFlags: Post-vote contributions from developers tied to approved projects - review timing in filings

districts.fundingNotes: Developer money shadows 'controlled growth' pitch. Pro-Neunder PAC 'Conservatives for a Brighter Future' got $95k from Pat Neal, Hugh Culverhouse, Randy Benderson, Rex Jensen. $10k from Pat Neal to PAC. Post-vote Siesta Key hotel/restaurant network (Chris Brown LLCs: Siesta Key Summer House, Ocean Blvd etc.) gave $7k before + $9k after waterfront property deal; refunded $4k. 2022 ~20% from devs. Land-use attorneys contributed. Siesta Key hotel operators in mix. Critics: symbol of SWFL political distrust.

districts.name: Florida Governor 2026 (Statewide - Local Impact on Growth & Marshlands)

incumbent.name: Open seat (Ron DeSantis term-limited)
incumbent.notes: Major impact on local development, taxes, environment, budget. Affects Sarasota/Manatee growth policies.

candidates.name: Byron Donalds (R frontrunner, Trump endorsed)
candidates.funding: ~$81M+ total raised (campaign + PACs as of mid-2026 reports); massive war chest from national GOP, business & donors

topContributors.name: Florida Chamber of Commerce PAC and other business groups

candidates.name: James Fishback (R - Gen Z / grassroots challenger)
candidates.status: Crowd favorite, polling underdog but rising
candidates.funding: Lower than Donalds; relies on small donors and events

topContributors.name: Small-dollar donors & online grassroots (hundreds of individual contributions under $200 via events/crowdfunding)
Limited large PACs; focus on self/small network per early filings (e.g., event-based from supporters)

candidates.name: David Jolly (D leading contender)
candidates.status: Leading Dem in general election polling

keyIssues.stance: Donalds: pro-business growth, limited regs; Fishback: more skeptical of sprawl, pro-local control
Governor sets tone via DEP/FDEP appointments, budget, veto power on growth bills impacting Sarasota/Manatee wetlands (e.g. Myakka, coastal buffers)
State budget and tax policy directly hits local infrastructure, schools, services funding
Fishback: anti-establishment, youth turnout, events over ads; Donalds: traditional GOP machine + Trump

districts.fundingNotes: Statewide race with massive local stakes for Sarasota/Manatee growth and marshlands. Donalds has overwhelming fundraising advantage (~$81M+ total raised via campaign + PACs as of mid-2026 reports; Q1 record $22M+, ~$65M+ cash on hand). Fishback emphasizes grassroots and has drawn large crowds (e.g. Merritt Island library standing-room only, mostly young; UCF events with hundreds). Polling vs Reality: Mainstream polls (AIF June 2026: ~54% Donalds/8% Fishback/5% Collins/2% Renner/31% undecided; Emerson April: 46% Donalds/4% Fishback/4% Collins/39% undecided) show big Donalds lead. HOWEVER: Fishback's events and small-donor metrics suggest stronger support among Gen Z/young voters and independents; crowds/turnout often outperform polls. HEAVY SKEPTICISM ON ALL POLLING — mainstream polls have been unreliable historically in FL races; cross-check with finance filings (dos.fl.gov), event size, small-dollar diversity, and local sentiment. No poll worship.
districts.pollingSources: AIF June 2026 (floridapolitics.com/archives/801165), Emerson College April 2026, Change Research, Florida Today (Merritt Island crowds May 2026), Ballotpedia

districts.name: Florida Commissioner of Agriculture 2026 (Statewide - Marshlands & Ag vs Development)

incumbent.name: Wilton Simpson (R)
incumbent.notes: Incumbent; key player in ag land protection, hemp rules, and balancing development pressure on wetlands/marshlands.

topContributors.name: Florida Farm Bureau PAC and agribusiness groups (major in filings)
Florida Chamber of Commerce PAC ($165k+ reported)

candidates.name: Matt 'The Welder' Taylor (Matt Taylor) (R primary challenger)
Kyle Gibson (NPA general)
candidates.status: No Party Affiliation general election candidate

keyIssues.stance: Simpson: 2026 Farm Bill + Rural Lands easements (100k+ acres protected, e.g. Charlie Creek Marsh) guard ag land/marsh buffers from sprawl; FDACS role in ag/water policy. Critics: some conservation provisions, not aggressive enough vs dev pressure in Sarasota/Manatee
Focus on 'guardrails' and cracking down on intoxicating products (7-OH, etc.)
Core tension for local: preserving farmland/wetlands (Myakka, etc.) or allowing growth/building

districts.fundingNotes: Critical local impact: FDACS policies on ag land preservation (Rural & Family Lands Protection Program easements) directly affect Sarasota/Manatee marshlands/rural buffers vs developer pressure. Simpson (5th-gen farmer, Trump-endorsed): 2026 Florida Farm Bill (his priority, signed Mar 2026) protects rural/conservation lands from high-density dev (unanimous local approval required); preserved 100k+ acres via easements (e.g. marsh areas). Voting record excerpts: Supported the Farm Bill with provisions for ag land protection and hemp guardrails; backed rural economic development bills. Primary challengers: Matt 'The Welder' Taylor (outsider, populist) and Richard Olle (low-profile). Dems Joey Atkins, Donald Prichard; NPA Kyle Gibson. Agribusiness/lobbyist funding common — watch development interests on land-use. Heavy focus for marshlands transparency. Simpson's agribusiness ties vs challengers' outsider appeal.
sources: local reporting on 2026 Farm Bill & Rural Lands easements (e.g. Charlie Creek Marsh)
